137.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token. This leads to incorrect output because the erroneous token should not be added to the token stream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token. This leads to incorrect output because the erroneous token should not be added to the token stream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token. This causes invalid tokens to be added to the token stream instead of being fully rejected.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token, which should not happen. The lexer should skip adding this invalid token to the token stream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and proceeds to add a token. This is incorrect because erroneous tokens should not produce valid tokens.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token, which should not happen. The lexer should skip adding this invalid token to the token stream.]

144.reason: [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.OR and proceeds to add it as a valid token, which is incorrect behavior for a lexical error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.OR and proceeds to add it as a valid token, which is incorrect behavior for a lexical error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported but TokenType.OR is still assigned, leading to incorrect inclusion of an invalid token in the output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.OR and will add it as a valid token. This is incorrect behavior for an erroneous symbol.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported but the lexer still assigns TokenType.OR and will output a token, which violates the specification that erroneous inputs should only produce error output and no tokens.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.OR and will output it as a valid token, which is incorrect. Invalid tokens should not be added to the token stream.]
